name: Web Reporting
description: Standard for a unified report substrate — one normalized model (sources, sections, findings, severities, scores, series) every producer targets so reports from different sources render through the same UI, with adapters that ingest (and emit) common report formats instead of bespoke viewers. Defines the report-model Protocol: multi-vendor report-format interop (SARIF, JUnit, coverage, SonarQube all exchange the same kind of artifact) is a genuine, escapable, impl-swappable contract — the normalized schema is the lock, and ingest/export adapters bridge each foreign format bottom-up into the pivot. A Project that owns one Protocol; an intent would be UX-only and wrong for a data-interchange contract. Distinct from a catalog page (which renders a registry): reporting renders findings/results over time. The output layer the conformance tooling, a future Web Compliance project, and external sources all render through.
